free_text: Gamma-ray production by proton and alpha-particle
induced reactions on C-12, O-16, Mg-24 and Fe.
production by inelastic proton scattering on O-16 and
The different targets were fixed onto brass frames
and arranged on a multiple target holder, which could
be shifted vertically to place the different materials
In additional to runs with the different targets which
lasted typically 30 min, runs for most beam energies
with an empty frame were used to determine part of the
The induced gamma-rays were detected by a
set of five high efficiency HP-Ge detectors with
bismuth germinate(BGO) scintillator shields for active
Compton suppression from the EUROGAM phase I setup.
In additional to runs with the different targets which
lasted typically 30 min, runs for most beam energies
with an empty frame were used to determine part of the
The gamma-ray fluxes at the different detector angles
were obtained with the dead-time-corrected line
Two procedures were used-simple integration of line
counts with subtraction of a background estimated from
count levels below and above the peak and a standard
fitting procedure with parametrized response of Ge
Both procedures were usually employed for narrow lines,
giving an estimate of the uncertainty due to the
background subtraction which was added quadratically
EN in data section is the energy in the center of the
<EN-ERR> The uncertainties on the projectile energy
correspond to half the energy loss in the target.
